engineGuide.h2: Ford EcoSport Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford EcoSport across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: Ford's award-winning three-cylinder turbocharged EcoBoost that became the most popular engine in the EcoSport range, but plagued by wet belt design flaws.
compatibleVariants: Style, Zetec, Titanium, Zetec S, Titanium S, Storm, Black Edition (all pre-facelift 125PS variants)
commonFailures: Wet timing belt degrades and sheds rubber particles into oil pickup strainer (60,000-80,000 miles)
Oil pressure warning due to blocked strainer leading to oil starvation
Complete engine seizure from oil starvation
entries.cta: Get quotes for M1JC Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: Higher-output 140PS version of the 1.0 EcoBoost, introduced in 2016 as a performance option for Titanium and ST-Line trims.
compatibleVariants: Titanium, ST-Line, Vignale, Black Edition (140PS variants), Active (late models)
commonFailures: Wet belt degradation (pre-2019) identical to 125PS units
Turbocharger oil seal failure from contaminated oil
Low oil pressure warning light (first symptom of belt failure)
entries.cta: Get quotes for YYJF Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: The revised post-2019 1.0 EcoBoost that replaced the problematic wet timing belt with a timing chain for camshafts, though the oil pump retained a wet belt.
compatibleVariants: Active, ST-Line, Vignale, Titanium (facelift models), Black Edition
commonFailures: Oil pump wet belt still vulnerable to degradation (reduced risk vs earlier engines)
Coolant loss from water pump seal failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for YJIF Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated four-cylinder Duratec-derived engine offered as a lower-cost alternative to the turbocharged EcoBoost, known for better reliability.
compatibleVariants: Style, Zetec, Titanium, Zetec S (pre-facelift 1.5 Ti-VCT variants)
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold cracking (reported in some units)
entries.cta: Get quotes for UEJB Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 6L Petrol
entries.history: Lower-output 90PS version of Ford's 1.6 Ti-VCT, primarily offered in entry-level trims for budget-conscious buyers.
commonFailures: Valve clearance adjustment required at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for HXJD Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 6L Petrol
entries.history: Higher-output 122PS version of the 1.6 Ti-VCT, offering more power than the 1.5 Ti-VCT with comparable naturally aspirated reliability.
compatibleVariants: Zetec, Titanium, Zetec S (1.6 Ti-VCT 122PS variants)
commonFailures: Oil consumption from piston ring wear (higher mileage)
entries.cta: Get quotes for IQJB Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: The only engine offered with all-wheel drive on the EcoSport, this naturally aspirated 2.0-litre Duratec was designed for 4x4 variants requiring extra low-end torque.
compatibleVariants: Titanium 4x4, Zetec 4x4, Storm 4x4 (2.0 petrol AWD models)
entries.cta: Get quotes for C20HDEA Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Diesel
entries.history: Ford's 1.5-litre Duratorq turbodiesel introduced in 2014, offering strong fuel economy and torque for EcoSport buyers wanting diesel efficiency.
compatibleVariants: Style, Zetec, Titanium, Trend (90PS TDCi variants)
entries.cta: Get quotes for UGJE Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Diesel
entries.history: Later refinement of the 1.5 Duratorq diesel, meeting stricter Euro emissions standards while maintaining the same power output.
entries.cta: Get quotes for XUJH Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Diesel
entries.history: Ford's next-generation EcoBlue diesel introduced in 2017, featuring selective catalytic reduction (SCR) with AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance and improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: Titanium, ST-Line, Active, Zetec (post-facelift 1.5 EcoBlue variants)
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues on short journeys
entries.cta: Get quotes for XVJD Ford EcoSport engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 6-compliant EcoBlue variant fitted to facelift EcoSport models, sharing architecture with XVJD but with different ECU calibration.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ZTJA Ford EcoSport engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Ford EcoSport Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost Wet Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost Wet Timing Belt Failure - The Most Common Cause of Engine Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost 125PS and 140PS (2013-2019, M1JC, M1JJ, M1JU, YYJF)
problems.rootCause: The wet belt runs inside the engine oil, which degrades over time and sheds rubber particles into the sump. These particles block the oil pickup strainer, causing oil starvation to the turbocharger, camshafts, and crankshaft bearings - often without warning before catastrophic seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, oil pump belt, water pump, tensioners, and full oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles - only viable if failure caught before oil pressure warning appears

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.0 EcoBoost with upgraded belts, new oil pump, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles - necessary once belt debris has blocked the pickup strainer

problems.recommendation: If your EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost shows no symptoms but has exceeded 60,000 miles or 7 years, preventative belt replacement through a specialist saves thousands versus waiting for failure. Once the oil pressure warning appears or the engine rattles, replacement is almost always the only option - dealer quotes around £7,200 versus specialist rebuilt units from £1,395 supply & fit. On a car worth £5,000-£12,000, replacement through our network makes financial sense.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M1JC and YYJF engine prices for your Ford EcoSport

problems.group: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost Oil Pressure Warning & Sudden Power Loss
problems.h4: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost Oil Pressure Warning & Sudden Power Loss - Signs of Imminent Failure
problems.affectedModels: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost 125PS and 140PS (2013-2019, M1JC, M1JJ, M1JU, YYJF)
problems.rootCause: Wet belt rubber particles accumulate in the oil pickup strainer, progressively restricting oil flow. The first symptom is often intermittent oil pressure warnings at idle or low speeds, followed by sudden power loss on motorways as the turbo is starved of oil.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Oil System Flush & Belt Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, clean pickup strainer, replace wet belts, oil pump, full system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Uncertain - engine may already have bearing damage even if running

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.0 EcoBoost with upgraded components, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles - recommended if oil pressure warnings have occurred multiple times

problems.recommendation: Once you see an oil pressure warning on a 1.0 EcoBoost, bearing damage may already have started. A simple belt change and strainer clean might buy time but carries risk. For most owners experiencing this symptom, we recommend budgeting for a full replacement - specialist rebuilt units start at £1,395 fitted, versus £6,500+ from a main dealer. The peace of mind from a warranted engine is worth the modest premium over a risky repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost replacement engines with warranty

problems.group: Ford EcoSport 1.5 TDCi Diesel Injector Failure
problems.h4: Ford EcoSport 1.5 TDCi Diesel Injector Failure - Common on Higher-Mileage Examples
problems.affectedModels: Ford EcoSport 1.5 TDCi (2014-2017, UGJE, XUJH, ZTJA)
problems.rootCause: Diesel injector solenoid or piezoelectric crystal failure leads to uneven fueling, misfires, white smoke from exhaust, and eventually hydro-lock or piston damage if a stuck-open injector fills a cylinder with diesel.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injectors, code new units to ECU, replace seals and copper washers
Replace all four injectors, fuel filter, high-pressure lines, ECU coding
Supply & fit rebuilt 1.5 TDCi with new injectors, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if injector failure caused piston or cylinder head damage

problems.recommendation: Injector failure on the 1.5 TDCi rarely writes off the engine unless ignored. A single failed injector can be replaced for £450-£800 at a specialist. However, if the car has covered over 100,000 miles and multiple injectors fail, a full rebuilt engine with new injectors becomes cost-effective - around £1,200-£2,500 fitted through our network versus dealer pricing above £4,500.
problems.cta: Compare 1.5 TDCi rebuilt engine prices for Ford EcoSport

problems.group: Ford EcoSport 1.5 Ti-VCT Catalytic Converter & Ignition Failure
problems.h4: Ford EcoSport 1.5 Ti-VCT Catalytic Converter & Ignition Failure - Petrol Naturally Aspirated Weak Points
problems.rootCause: The 1.5 Ti-VCT is generally reliable, but ignition coil packs degrade with age leading to misfires. Unburnt fuel from misfires then overheats and destroys the catalytic converter - a costly secondary failure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Ignition Coil & Spark Plug Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four ignition coils and spark plugs, clear fault codes
Replace catalytic converter, oxygen sensors, address root cause misfire
Supply & fit rebuilt 1.5 Ti-VCT, new ignition components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only if engine damaged from prolonged misfire or oil starvation

problems.recommendation: On a 1.5 Ti-VCT, catch ignition problems early. A £200-£350 coil pack replacement prevents a £600-£1,000 catalytic converter failure. The engine itself is robust - replacement is rarely needed unless the car was driven for months with a misfire. If you do need a replacement, specialist rebuilt units from £1,100 offer far better value than dealer pricing above £3,500.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Ford EcoSport 1.5 Ti-VCT engines - UEJB and UEJE codes

problems.group: Ford EcoSport 1.5 EcoBlue DPF & AdBlue System Failure
problems.h4: Ford EcoSport 1.5 EcoBlue DPF & AdBlue System Failure - Diesel Post-2017 Weaknesses
problems.rootCause: The Euro 6 EcoBlue diesel relies on frequent DPF regenerations and AdBlue injection. Short journeys prevent regeneration, leading to DPF blockage. AdBlue crystallisation in the injector and tank heater failure are common design weaknesses.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced regeneration using diagnostic equipment, cleaning DPF with additive
repairOptions.longevity: 10,000-20,000 miles - only works if blockage is light

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Removal & Cleaning or AdBlue Repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and professionally clean DPF OR replace AdBlue injector/tank heater
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000+ miles after cleaning; AdBlue repairs variable

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.5 EcoBlue, new DPF, AdBlue system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if DPF back-pressure damaged turbo or EGR

problems.recommendation: The 1.5 EcoBlue is reliable if used for regular long runs. For urban driving, reconsider diesel ownership. If you already own one with DPF problems, a forced regeneration at a specialist costs as little as £80. For AdBlue system failures, expect £400-£800 at a specialist versus £1,500 at a dealer. Full engine replacement is rare on this unit unless the problem was ignored for tens of thousands of miles.
problems.cta: Compare XVJD and ZTJA rebuilt engine prices for Ford EcoSport

problems.h4: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost Turbocharger Failure - Caused by Wet Belt Contamination
problems.affectedModels: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost 125PS and 140PS (2013-2019, M1JC, M1JJ, M1JU, YYJF)
problems.rootCause: The turbocharger is the first component to suffer when wet belt debris blocks the oil pickup strainer. Oil starvation destroys the turbo bearings, often before the engine itself seizes - but turbo failure is a warning sign of wider oil system contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger, oil feed pipes, drain lines, oil change
repairOptions.longevity: High risk - contaminated oil will destroy new turbo within months

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.0 EcoBoost with new turbo, oil pump, belts, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles - only reliable solution once turbo has failed

problems.recommendation: Never replace just the turbo on a 1.0 EcoBoost that has suffered turbo failure. The same contaminated oil that destroyed the original turbo will kill the replacement within 5,000-10,000 miles. We see this mistake constantly. The correct fix is a complete rebuilt engine with new turbo, oil pump, and belts. Through our network, this costs £1,600-£3,000 fitted - less than a dealer would charge for just a turbo replacement. Do it once, do it properly.
problems.cta: Get Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost rebuilt engine quotes with new turbo included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford EcoSport
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your EcoSport suffers from the well-documented wet belt failure on the 1.0 EcoBoost, injector problems on the 1.5 TDCi, or DPF issues on the 1.5 EcoBlue, replacement through our specialist network costs a fraction of main dealer prices. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Enter your reg number above - compare prices from trusted UK Ford EcoSport specialists and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford EcoSport Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford EcoSport and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Ford EcoSport Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDisclaimer: Not all engine codes available recon - check compatibility
types.backDescription: Reconditioned units are stripped, inspected and rebuilt to factory tolerances using new OEM-grade pistons, rings, bearings and seals. Ideal for EcoSport owners wanting OEM reliability without dealer pricing.
backBullets: For M1JC, YYJF and UGJE engine codes
Bench tested before dispatch - 7-14 day turnaround

types.title: Ford EcoSport Rebuilt Engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Lower cost than recon but fewer new components
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines focus on restoring function rather than returning to factory-new specification. Popular for 1.0 EcoBoost owners needing cost-effective replacement after wet belt failure.

6-12 month warranty - 5-10 day typical lead time

types.backDescription: The most budget-friendly option for EcoSport owners with seized 1.0 EcoBoost engines needing a quick replacement. Best suited to older vehicles with lower residual value.

types.backDescription: Every component is measured and machined to factory service limits. Remanufacturing addresses the original wet belt weakness with upgraded oil pump and belt components.

Upgraded wet belt or timing chain conversion for 1.0 EcoBoost

types.title: Ford EcoSport Refurbished Engines

types.backDescription: A cosmetic and light mechanical refresh for running engines with external leaks or tired ancillaries. Not suitable for engines with internal failure or oil pressure issues.

engineSizes.h2: Ford EcoSport Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Below you will find every displacement fitted to the Ford EcoSport in the UK, with compatible engine codes, years, and common failure notes.

items.title: Ford EcoSport 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre TDCi (Duratorq) diesel engine was a key powertrain for the first-generation EcoSport sold in the UK from 2013 onwards, offering an economical alternative to the petrol units. It was later replaced by the cleaner 1.5-litre EcoBlue diesel after the 2017 facelift, which added AdBlue technology for Euro 6 compliance. This engine size became the primary diesel option for UK buyers, known for strong torque delivery suited to the EcoSport's SUV body style.
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford EcoSport 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Ford EcoSport 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0-litre EcoBoost engine is the most recognised and widely sold petrol unit in the Ford EcoSport range. Launched globally to critical acclaim, it was the first SUV in the world to be offered with a 1.0-litre engine, using turbocharging and direct injection to replace larger 1.6-litre naturally aspirated units. In the UK, it was available in 125 PS and later 140 PS outputs. While early versions (pre-2019) utilised a "wet belt" timing system that proved problematic, later models switched to a timing chain, improving long-term reliability.
commonFailurePoints: oil pressure loss due to blocked strainer
items.cta: Compare Ford EcoSport 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford EcoSport 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre Ti-VCT engine served as the naturally aspirated petrol option in the early EcoSport lineup, particularly favoured in markets where the EcoBoost was considered too premium. It is a 4-cylinder unit (later revised to a 3-cylinder in some markets) offering a simpler, non-turbocharged driving experience. While less powerful than the smaller EcoBoost engine, it was often chosen by UK buyers for its perceived lower long-term maintenance complexity, though it has become less common in later model years.
items.cta: Compare Ford EcoSport 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford EcoSport 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre Ti-VCT engine was more common in earlier global markets for the EcoSport but did appear in the UK range, offering a larger displacement alternative to the 1.0 EcoBoost and 1.5 Ti-VCT. It provided a balance between the efficiency of the smaller turbo engine and the raw displacement of the larger 2.0-litre unit. It was typically found in higher-spec variants before the facelift models standardised the 1.5-litre options.
items.cta: Compare Ford EcoSport 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford EcoSport 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Duratec engine represents the largest displacement available for the Ford EcoSport, typically reserved for higher-spec trims. In specific export markets and available in the UK primarily on earlier models or specific 4x4 variants, this engine offered the highest power output in the range. It is a naturally aspirated unit that provides linear power delivery suitable for the heavier AWD (All-Wheel Drive) versions of the EcoSport, though it remains a rarer find on UK roads compared to the 1.0 EcoBoost. Not sure which engine size your Ford EcoSport needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford EcoSport specialists.
items.cta: Compare Ford EcoSport 2.0L petrol engine prices

Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. fuelTypes.h2: Ford EcoSport Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Ford EcoSport engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the early 1.5 TDCi diesel to the latest 1.0 EcoBoost petrol.

items.title: Ford EcoSport Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power arrived with the 1.5 TDCi Duratorq in 2014, later replaced by the cleaner 1.5 EcoBlue after the 2017 facelift - but neither dominates replacement volumes like the petrol EcoBoost.
items.descriptor: Diesel power arrived with the 1.5 TDCi Duratorq in 2014, later replaced by the cleaner 1.5 EcoBlue after the 2017 facelift - but neither dominates replacement volumes like the petrol EcoBoost.
families: UGJE - 1.5 TDCi 90PS, main diesel from 2014-2017, injector failure common
XVJD - 1.5 EcoBlue 95PS, post-2017 facelift, AdBlue system issues
ZTJA - 1.5 EcoBlue, final Euro 6 version for 2017-2023 models
XUJH - 1.5 TDCi, transitional unit with DPF sensitivity
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy (55-60mpg realistic)
Injector failure on higher-mileage 1.5 TDCi units (80k+ miles)
DPF blockage on short journeys - needs regular regeneration runs
AdBlue crystallisation problems on post-2017 EcoBlue variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford EcoSport Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Ford EcoSport Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol powers the vast majority of UK EcoSport replacements, with the 1.0 EcoBoost dominating both sales and failure enquiries - the wet belt crisis has made this the most searched engine in the range.
items.descriptor: Petrol powers the vast majority of UK EcoSport replacements, with the 1.0 EcoBoost dominating both sales and failure enquiries - the wet belt crisis has made this the most searched engine in the range.
families: M1JC/M1JJ/M1JU - 1.0 EcoBoost 125PS, wet belt, highest replacement volume
YYJF - 1.0 EcoBoost 140PS performance version, same wet belt weakness
YJIF - 1.0 EcoBoost chain-driven post-2019, reduced failure risk
UEJB/UEJE - 1.5 Ti-VCT 110-112PS, naturally aspirated, more reliable
IQJB - 1.6 Ti-VCT 122PS, larger NA option for pre-facelift
foundIn: B515 pre-facelift (2013-2017) - M1JC, YYJF, UEJB, IQJB
B515 facelift (2017-2023) - YJIF chain engine, YYJF carried over
knownFor: Wet timing belt degradation on pre-2019 1.0 EcoBoost (60k-80k miles)
Oil starvation from blocked pickup strainer - sudden failure
Turbocharger failure from contaminated oil supply
Post-2019 chain engines significantly more reliable
Naturally aspirated Ti-VCT units have fewer catastrophic failures
items.cta: Compare Ford EcoSport Petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford EcoSport Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: Ford never offered a hybrid, plug-in hybrid, or fully electric version of the EcoSport in the UK market.
items.descriptor: Ford never offered a hybrid, plug-in hybrid, or fully electric version of the EcoSport in the UK market.
importantNotes: No mild hybrid (MHEV) EcoSport models were sold in the UK
No plug-in hybrid (PHEV) variant exists for this model
The replacement EcoSport (Puma) introduced mild hybrid technology, but the EcoSport itself remained conventional
All UK EcoSport engine replacements are for standard petrol or diesel units only

engineYears.h2: Ford EcoSport Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Ford EcoSport's UK production timeline from 2013 to 2023, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The EcoSport arrived in UK showrooms in 2013, introducing the now-famous 1.0 EcoBoost with its controversial wet belt design.
keyChanges: Launch of Ford's first compact SUV in the UK market
1.5 Ti-VCT naturally aspirated petrol available for lower-budget buyers
Rear-mounted spare wheel (the "backpack") a signature styling feature
knownFor: Most searched EcoSport generation for engine replacement (wet belt failure peak)
Wet timing belt degrades between 60,000-80,000 miles
Oil starvation from blocked pickup strainer is primary failure mode
1.5 Ti-VCT and 1.6 Ti-VCT naturally aspirated units are more reliable
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2017 Ford EcoSport replacement engines
years.ticker: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost engine replacement • M1JC engine failure wet belt • Ford EcoSport 2014 engine seized • 1.5 TDCi injector failure EcoSport

years.description: The facelift brought styling updates and SYNC3 infotainment, but the 1.0 EcoBoost retained its wet belt - meaning problems continued for another two years.
keyChanges: Mid-life facelift with revised front grille and headlamps
1.5 Ti-VCT and 1.6 Ti-VCT petrol engines phased out
Rear styling updated but spare wheel remains
mainEngines: YYJF - 1.0 EcoBoost 140PS petrol (continued, still wet belt)
ZTJA - 1.5 EcoBlue diesel variant for facelift models
knownFor: Wet belt vulnerability unchanged - same failures as 2013-2017
EcoBlue diesel adds AdBlue system crystallisation risks
ST-Line trim introduced with sportier suspension and styling
Popular with younger buyers and first-time SUV owners
Owners still caught out by belt failure with no warning recall from Ford
Replacement demand remains high despite facelift improvements
years.cta: Compare 2017-2019 Ford EcoSport engine prices - wet belt models
years.ticker: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost wet belt problem • YYJF engine failure symptoms • 1.5 EcoBlue DPF issues • Ford EcoSport ST-Line engine replacement cost
enquiries: Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost wet belt problem
Ford EcoSport ST-Line engine replacement cost

years.description: Ford finally addressed the wet belt crisis by switching the 1.0 EcoBoost to a timing chain for the camshafts - though the oil pump retained a wet belt.
keyChanges: 1.0 EcoBoost cambelt replaced with timing chain (major reliability improvement)
Production continued until 2023 when EcoSport was discontinued
Final years saw trim rationalisation before model ended
2.0 petrol discontinued after 2017, not available in this period
knownFor: Chain-driven camshafts significantly reduce failure risk vs wet belt
Oil pump still uses wet belt - some residual risk remains
Most reliable EcoSport petrol engines in the entire production run
Final production year 2023 - model discontinued without direct replacement
Vignale trim offers highest specification and luxury features
Replacement demand lower than pre-2019 models but still significant
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2023 Ford EcoSport chain-driven engine replacements
years.ticker: Ford EcoSport 2019 chain engine reliability • YJIF engine replacement cost • Ford EcoSport 2020 1.0 EcoBoost prices • Last year EcoSport engine availability

faq.h2: Ford EcoSport Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost wet belt failure really that common?
items.answer: Yes - the wet timing belt on pre-2019 1.0 EcoBoost engines is the most common cause of catastrophic engine failure on the EcoSport.
keyPoints: Wet belt degrades from oil and fuel contamination, shedding rubber into the sump
Debris blocks the oil pickup strainer, causing oil starvation
Typical failure between 60,000-80,000 miles
UK has over 700,000 vehicles with this engine - failure rate is significant
Ford issued recalls and service messages acknowledging the problem
items.cta: Compare pre-2019 EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost replacement engines with warranty

items.question: How much does a Ford EcoSport engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Specialist rebuilt engines cost £1,195-£2,800 supply only, plus £200-£600 fitting. Main dealers charge £5,600-£7,242 for the same job.
keyPoints: Dealer engine replacement recorded at £7,242 - most expensive repair for this model
Specialist rebuilt 1.0 EcoBoost from £1,395 supply and fitted
Used engines cheaper but limited warranty (30-90 days typically)
YYJF 1.0 140PS replacement engine averages £2,270
Average repair cost across all issues is £1,130
items.cta: Get instant Ford EcoSport engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: What are the symptoms of Ford EcoSport wet belt failure before it fails completely?
items.answer: Loss of power on motorways, intermittent oil pressure warnings, rattling from the engine, and eventually a complete loss of oil pressure.
keyPoints: "All of a sudden, I got a warning light appear... the power just dropped" - real owner experience
Rattling or knocking noises from timing cover area
Check engine light with timing correlation codes (P0016/P0017)
Some failures occur with full service history at just 50,000 miles
Low oil pressure warning at idle is the critical last warning
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost engines before your current one fails

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Ford EcoSport?
items.answer: Yes - with used EcoSport values at £5,000-£12,000 and specialist replacement costing £1,395-£2,800 fitted, replacement makes financial sense.
keyPoints: Dealer replacement (£7,242) often exceeds vehicle value - specialist replacement is cost-effective
Rebuilt engine includes 12-month warranty - often longer than a used car warranty
EcoSport has no direct replacement in Ford's current UK lineup
Many owners have emotional attachment or vehicle modifications that make retention worthwhile
Compare £1,395 specialist fit vs buying another used EcoSport with potential hidden issues
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine prices for your Ford EcoSport - save thousands vs dealer

items.question: Which Ford EcoSport engines are most reliable?
items.answer: The post-2019 1.0 EcoBoost with timing chain is most reliable petrol. The 1.5 TDCi diesel is reliable if used for regular long journeys.
keyPoints: 2019+ 1.0 EcoBoost (chain-driven camshafts) - wet belt only on oil pump now
1.5 TDCi diesel - robust but needs long runs for DPF health
Naturally aspirated 1.5 Ti-VCT and 1.6 Ti-VCT - fewer catastrophic failures
Owner reports 1.5 TDCi reaching 120,000+ miles with only routine servicing
Avoid pre-2019 1.0 EcoBoost with wet belt unless belt has been changed
items.cta: Find a reliable reconditioned Ford EcoSport engine for your specific variant

items.question: What causes the Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost to lose power suddenly?
items.answer: Wet belt debris blocks the oil pickup strainer, starving the turbocharger and timing components of oil - power drops as the engine protects itself.
keyPoints: Oil pump belt tensioner fails, belt shreds teeth into oil pan
Debris clogs pickup strainer - oil pressure drops
Turbo is first component to fail from oil starvation
NHTSA investigated 240,000 EcoSport vehicles for this issue
Some failures reported at just 50,000 miles - well below 150,000-mile service interval
items.cta: Get Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost replacement quotes with upgraded components

items.question: How long do Ford EcoSport engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained 1.5 TDCi diesels can exceed 120,000-180,000 miles. Pre-2019 1.0 EcoBoost often fails between 50,000-80,000 miles without preventative belt change.
keyPoints: 1.5 TDCi owner reports "engine would last more than 3 lakh km (186,000 miles)"
Average mileage at failure recorded at 60,100 miles
Wet belt service interval is 150,000 miles - but failures occur much earlier
Regular oil changes with correct 5W20 specification critical
Post-2019 chain-driven engines expected to last significantly longer
items.cta: Compare Ford EcoSport engine options by longevity and warranty coverage

items.question: Can I fit a Ford Fiesta 1.0 EcoBoost engine to my Ford EcoSport?
items.answer: Yes - the 1.0 EcoBoost engine is shared between EcoSport, Fiesta and Focus, but you must match the engine code precisely for compatibility.
keyPoints: Same M1JC/M1JJ/M1JU/YJIF codes appear across all three models
Check your specific engine code before purchasing - codes must match
ECU mapping may differ - use your original ECU or have it reprogrammed
Some ancillaries (mounts, pipes) may differ between models
Specialist supplier will verify compatibility before sale
items.cta: Get matched quotes for Ford EcoSport 1.0 EcoBoost engines - correct code guaranteed

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt, and used engines for Ford EcoSport?
items.answer: Reconditioned = factory-spec rebuild with new parts. Rebuilt = restored to working order. Used = removed from donor vehicle - cheapest but least warranty.
keyPoints: Used engines: £950-£1,800, 30-90 day warranty - budget option for older cars
Rebuilt engines: £1,195-£2,500, new gaskets, rings and bearings - 6-12 month warranty
Reconditioned: £1,400-£2,800, OEM-spec with new components - 12-24 month warranty
Supply & fit adds £200-£600 - recommended for non-DIY owners
Every engine through our network has minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare all Ford EcoSport engine condition types and prices side by side

items.question: Does the Ford EcoSport have a timing belt or timing chain?
items.answer: Pre-2019 1.0 EcoBoost uses a wet timing belt (replaced at 150,000 miles or 10 years). Post-2019 switched to timing chain for camshafts. Diesel engines use timing belts.
keyPoints: 2013-2019 1.0 EcoBoost - wet timing belt (major failure risk)
2019-2023 1.0 EcoBoost - timing chain for camshafts (more reliable)
1.5 TDCi and 1.5 EcoBlue - conventional timing belt (replace at 100,000 miles)
1.5/1.6 Ti-VCT petrol - timing belt (conventional, less problematic than EcoBoost wet belt)
Ford reduced wet belt replacement recommendation from 150,000 to 100,000 miles proactively
items.cta: Find out what timing system your Ford EcoSport has - get compatible replacement quotes
